For the font to display properly, you might need to include the Playfair Display font in your project. If it's not already included, you could add it to your global CSS or layout file by adding this line:

```css
@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Playfair+Display:wght@300;400;500&display=swap');
```

_**Assistant (copilot/claude-3.7-sonnet)**_

Looking at your Flagship Events component, I can see that you want to change the background color of the event cards to match the blue gradient color of the image containers.

Currently, your image containers have a blue gradient background, but the card backgrounds are white. Let's modify the CSS to apply the same gradient to the card backgrounds:

```css
/* ...existing code... */

.eventCard {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 40px;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #4dd0e1 0%, #26c6da 50%, #00acc1 100%);
  border-radius: 20px;
  padding: 40px;
  box-shadow: 0 10px 30px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.08);
  transition: transform 0.3s ease, box-shadow 0.3s ease;
}

/* Update text colors since background is now blue */
.eventTitle {
  font-size: 1.8rem;
  font-weight: 600;
  color: white;
  margin: 0 0 16px 0;
  line-height: 1.3;
}

.eventDate {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 8px;
  margin-bottom: 24px;
  font-size: 0.95rem;
  color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.9);
}

.eventDescription {
  font-size: 1rem;
  line-height: 1.6;
  color: white;
  margin: 0 0 20px 0;
  text-align: justify;
}

.additionalText {
  font-size: 1rem;
  line-height: 1.6;
  color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.9);
  margin: 0;
  font-style: italic;
  text-align: justify;
}

/* ...existing code... */
```

This change will:
1. Apply the same blue gradient background to the event cards that's currently used in the image containers
2. Update the text colors to white or light colors to ensure readability against the new blue background
3. Keep the box shadow and hover effects that were commented out before

If you'd like to test these changes in your browser to see how they look, you can run:

```
"$BROWSER" http://localhost:3000
```

(assuming your development server is running on port 3000)
